ksa_text: Document analytical methodologies, data sources, findings, business rules, and transformation logic, including producing documentation that meets evidence standards for regulatory compliance, audit, and executive decision support.

dcwf.top_element_label: Skill in conducting audits or reviews of technical systems.

nice.top_element_label: Knowledge of evidence admissibility laws and regulations

nist_ai_rmf.top_element_label: Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively.

top_anchors.element_label: Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively.
Skill in conducting audits or reviews of technical systems.

pass_2_evaluation.rationale: KSA AB-T-010 (Core Abilities/Task) scores 0.435 composite (highest: EU AI Act at 0.435) and is edge_case for the Data Engineer role. The KSA is an edge case for Core Abilities but contains 1 DE pattern signals sufficient to warrant inclusion for a senior IC data engineer. Seniority is appropriate for IC-to-senior IC data engineering.

pass_3_adversarial_internal.ruling: No inconsistency identified. Include consistent with edge_case scope, EU AI Act signal (0.435), and Core Abilities handling. Decision stands.

pass_4_adversarial_external.challenge: Does market evidence support this Core Ability as a distinct Data Engineer competency at IC/senior IC level?
pass_4_adversarial_external.evidence: General abilities appear implicitly in DE postings but rarely as discrete competencies. When explicit, they appear in context of pipeline design, data modeling, or quality enforcement (DE signals here: 1, composite: 0.435).
pass_4_adversarial_external.ruling: Revised to exclude. General ability without sufficient DE-specific context is not a distinct market requirement. Final decision: exclude.

decision_rationale: Revised to exclude. General ability without sufficient DE-specific context is not a distinct market requirement. Final decision: exclude.
